paper_map.title: Phase transformation and austenite stability during thermomechanical processing of high (~5%) Al added low-density medium Mn steel
paper_map.abstract_summary: A high Al (~5 wt.%) added low-density medium Mn steel (Fe-8Mn-5Al-0.2C nominal) was processed by casting, hot forging, hot rolling and intercritical annealing at 750 °C for 5 to 180 min. The evolution of phases (δ-ferrite, austenite, martensite, reverted austenite, intercritical ferrite) and their morphologies were studied, with tensile properties evaluated in relation to austenite stability. The specimen annealed for 60 min showed optimal mechanical stability of reverted austenite, resulting in an enhanced TRIP effect and the best combination of ultimate tensile strength (658±6 MPa) and total elongation (12±1%).
paper_map.research_objective: To develop a high Al low-density medium Mn steel and understand the phase transformation, austenite stability and deformation mechanisms as a function of thermomechanical processing and intercritical annealing time, and to correlate them with tensile properties.
main_process_variables: hot forging at 1050–800 °C, 50% thickness reduction, air cooling
hot rolling: soaking at 1100 °C for 2 h, rolling at 1050–800 °C in two passes with 50% reduction per pass, water quenching
intercritical annealing at 750 °C for 5, 30, 60, 120, 180 min, followed by air cooling
cooling rate (air cooling vs. water quenching)
sample_aliases: IA750_5 Min (intercritically annealed at 750 °C for 5 min)
expected_information_axis: property (tensile YS, UTS, TE, TRIP effect)
figure_evidence (micrographs, fractographs, phase diagrams, stress-strain curves)

key_sections.purpose: motivate high Al medium Mn steels, review TRIP/TWIP and phase stability factors, state aims
describe alloy production, thermomechanical processing, heat treatments and characterization
present and interpret alloy design, microstructural evolution, mechanical properties and austenite stability
summarize key findings on phase transformation, optimal annealing and corresponding tensile properties
